Tests for a ReferenceError when evaluating a specific JavaScript switch statement.

# Purpose
This code is a test script that checks the behavior of the JavaScript `eval` function when it encounters a syntax error. It attempts to evaluate a string containing a `switch` statement with an undefined variable `foo`, followed by an empty block. The script expects this to throw a `ReferenceError` due to the use of the undeclared variable `foo`. The `assert` function is used to verify that the error thrown is indeed a `ReferenceError`, ensuring that the JavaScript engine correctly handles this type of error.
